title: Saptak Festival of Music

infobox.value: [Indian classical music](/wiki/Indian_classical_music "Indian classical music") ([Hindustani](/wiki/Hindustani_music "Hindustani music"), [Carnatic](/wiki/Carnatic_music "Carnatic music"), [Ghazals](/wiki/Ghazals "Ghazals") etc.)
* [Nandan Mehta](/wiki/Nandan_Mehta "Nandan Mehta") * Manju Mehta * Rupande Shah * Bharti Parikh * D.D.Trivedi * Hetal Mehta

This article about the [music of India](/wiki/Music_of_India "Music of India") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Saptak_Annual_Festival_of_Music&action=edit).

is an annual thirteen-day [Indian classical music](/wiki/Indian_classical_music "Indian classical music") festival held in [Ahmedabad](/wiki/Ahmedabad "Ahmedabad"), [India](/wiki/India "India"). It is organised by [Saptak School of Music](/wiki/Saptak_School_of_Music "Saptak School of Music") and takes place January first to the thirteenth every year, and is attended by hundreds of people.[[1]](#cite_note-toik2010-1)[[2]](#cite_note-dna_2012-2)

["Saptak at 31: Music gods return to city"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160414024750/http://epaper.timesofindia.com/Repository/getFiles.asp?Style=OliveXLib:LowLevelEntityToPrint_TOI&Type=text/html&Locale=english-skin-custom&Path=TOIA/2010/12/24&ID=Ar00200). *epaper.timesofindia.com*. 24 December 2010. Archived from [the original](http://epaper.timesofindia.com/Repository/getFiles.asp?Style=OliveXLib:LowLevelEntityToPrint_TOI&Type=text/html&Locale=english-skin-custom&Path=TOIA/2010/12/24&ID=Ar00200) on 14 April 2016. Retrieved 4 April 2016.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Saptak Festival of Music](/w/index.php?title=Saptak_Festival_of_Music&redirect=no "Saptak Festival of Music"))

| Saptak Annual Music Festival | |
| --- | --- |
| Genre | [Indian classical music](/wiki/Indian_classical_music "Indian classical music") ([Hindustani](/wiki/Hindustani_music "Hindustani music"), [Carnatic](/wiki/Carnatic_music "Carnatic music"), [Ghazals](/wiki/Ghazals "Ghazals") etc.) |
| Dates | 1–13 January |
| Locations | [Ahmedabad](/wiki/Ahmedabad "Ahmedabad"), [India](/wiki/India "India") |
| Years active | 1980–present |
| Founders | * [Nandan Mehta](/wiki/Nandan_Mehta "Nandan Mehta") * Manju Mehta * Rupande Shah * Bharti Parikh * D.D.Trivedi * Hetal Mehta |
| Website | [Saptak](http://saptak.org/) |

The **Saptak Annual Festival of Music** is an annual thirteen-day [Indian classical music](/wiki/Indian_classical_music "Indian classical music") festival held in [Ahmedabad](/wiki/Ahmedabad "Ahmedabad"), [India](/wiki/India "India"). It is organised by [Saptak School of Music](/wiki/Saptak_School_of_Music "Saptak School of Music") and takes place January first to the thirteenth every year, and is attended by hundreds of people.[[1]](#cite_note-toik2010-1)[[2]](#cite_note-dna_2012-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saptak_Annual_Festival_of_Music&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The festival started in 1980 by [Nandan Mehta](/wiki/Nandan_Mehta "Nandan Mehta") and his wife Manju Mehta, Rupande Shah, Bharti Parikh and D.D. Trivedi, with the inauguration by Pandit [Ravi Shankar](/wiki/Ravi_Shankar "Ravi Shankar") and Pandit [Kishan Maharaj](/wiki/Kishan_Maharaj "Kishan Maharaj").[[2]](#cite_note-dna_2012-2) The one-day event in 1980 expanded to the performance of more than 130 musicians in 15 sessions spread across 13 days by 2010.[[3]](#cite_note-dnax-3)

The festival features both emerging talents and established performers. Besides pure classical music, it also includes performances of [folk music](/wiki/Indian_folk_music "Indian folk music"), [classical dance forms](/wiki/Indian_classical_dance "Indian classical dance"), and semi-classical forms such as [thumri](/wiki/Thumri "Thumri").[[3]](#cite_note-dnax-3)

## Gallery

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saptak_Annual_Festival_of_Music&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Gallery")]

The gallery dedicated to the life of Nandan Mehta and the history of festival was opened in Ahmedabad in 2012.[[2]](#cite_note-dna_2012-2)
